title: carex maritima subsp. capillipes
intro: Carex maritima subsp. capillipes, also known as the sea sedge, is a subspecies of grass-like perennial plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of Carex maritima subsp. capillipes include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for various health concerns. Carex maritima subsp. capillipes can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
